text.title: Cost-vs-Quality Decision-Log Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that turn a decision log from a graveyard of forgotten Slack threads into a queryable institutional memory.

rule.statement: The log is append-only. Past entries are never edited (except corrections marked inline). A reversal is a NEW entry that references and supersedes the prior one. Editing history erases the "why" that prevented re-litigation.
rule.rationale: Editable logs become whatever the current author wants them to say. Append-only preserves the moment-of-decision context, which is the only thing that protects against repeating bad trades.

rule.statement: Each entry MUST identify the feature scope (specific pipeline / endpoint / agent), not the platform. "Switched to sonnet" is rejected; "Quote endpoint switched from opus to sonnet for line-item extraction" is accepted. Cross-feature decisions get multiple entries.

rationale.source: Field practice from teams running mixed LLM stacks

rule.rationale: Platform-wide decisions hide feature-specific failures. A change that's right for one endpoint may be catastrophic for another. Feature-scoped entries surface the difference.

rule.statement: Each entry MUST define a rollback trigger: a measurable signal that would reverse the decision (e.g., "quality_score < 0.82 sustained over 7 days" or "complaints/month > 5"). Without a trigger, the decision becomes permanent by inertia regardless of impact.
rule.rationale: Decisions made under cost pressure default to "fine" if no one watches for degradation. The trigger is the watchful eye.

rule.statement: Each entry MUST have a review-after date (typical: 30 days for high-impact, 90 days for low-impact). On that date the entry status moves to "ratified" (kept as-is, with observed-impact note) or "reverted" (new entry written). Reviews are not optional; the script enumerates overdue entries.
rule.rationale: Without scheduled review, "temporary" cost cuts become permanent quality losses. The review date forces a deliberate keep-or-reverse decision.

rule.statement: Entries based on eval results MUST link to the corresponding cost-quality Pareto memo + fixture version. Entries based on observation MUST link to the monitoring dashboard or log query. Entries based on intuition are marked "intuition" — allowed for low-stakes trades but flagged for revisit.
rule.rationale: A decision without provenance cannot be re-examined. Linking to the Pareto/monitoring makes the log queryable evidence.

example.title: Well-formed log entry
example.code: ID: dec-2026-014 Date: 2026-05-15 Feature: quote-endpoint / line-item-extraction Decision: Switch model opus -> sonnet, cached system prompt Reason: Pareto memo memo-2026-05-10 — 0.04 quality gap vs 4.8x cost saving Trigger to revert: extraction_F1 < 0.82 over 7 days OR > 3 customer complaints / month Review-after: 2026-06-15 (30 days) Provenance: link to fixtures-v3, monitoring dashboard `quote-extraction-quality` Status: active Supersedes: none
example.note: Feature-scoped, rollback trigger explicit, review date set, link to Pareto.

example.title: Loose entry
example.code: "2026-05-15: dropped to sonnet, save money, looks fine"
example.note: No feature scope, no trigger, no review date, no provenance. Violates r2, r3, r4, r5.
